body:
_🧹 Nitpick_

**Early returns violate “single return” preference.**

Not a blocker. Consider consolidating for consistency with repo style.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/OS.ts around lines 144 to 149, the method htmlSrcPathToURI uses multiple
early returns (return html) for null checks; refactor to a single return at the
end by introducing a local variable (e.g., result = html), perform the checks
and only overwrite result when webviewPanel and webview exist and conversion is
applied, then return result once at the end; ensure behavior and short-circuit
logic remain identical and update any related flow comments.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_

**Now: 0.4× line-height will overlap lines — use ≥1.3×**

At 12px, 0.4× → ~5px leading. That will collide glyphs. Bump to ~1.4× (or clamp ≥ fontSize).

Apply:

```diff
-    return Math.round(fontSize * 0.4); // Tight line spacing for code printing
+    // Use readable leading for code; keep integer px
+    return Math.max(fontSize, Math.round(fontSize * 1.4));
```

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
    // Use readable leading for code; keep integer px
    return Math.max(fontSize, Math.round(fontSize * 1.4));
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/PaperPrinter.ts around line 191, the function returns Math.round(fontSize
* 0.4) which produces overlapping lines; change the computed line-height to use
a multiplicative factor of about 1.4 and clamp it to at least the fontSize
(e.g., leading = round(fontSize * 1.4); if leading < fontSize then leading =
fontSize) and update the inline comment to reflect the larger, readable leading
for code printing.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🧹 Nitpick_

**What: New toolbar items OK, but unify icon source**

Menus ‘page’/‘orient’ added. Consider sourcing toolbar icons from the same YAML to avoid emoji fallback drift.



Also applies to: 224-236

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/PaperPrinter.ts around lines 218-222 (and also apply same change to
224-236), the new toolbar items hardcode icon values causing divergence from the
project's YAML icon source; update these toolbar item definitions to read their
icon strings from the shared icons YAML/config loader (use the same key name
used elsewhere), falling back to the existing literal only if the YAML lookup
returns undefined, and ensure binding remains unchanged so menuItems and
selectionHandler continue to reference the same methods.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🧹 Nitpick_

**Sup: Page menu entries OK**

Clear labels with correct units. Consider sorting by locale default first later.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/PaperPrinter.ts around lines 335 to 344, the page menu currently returns
a hardcoded list unsorted; update it to prefer the locale-default paper size
first: detect the user's locale (e.g., from navigator.language in browser or
process.env.LANG/Intl API in node), map common locales to a preferred size
(e.g., US/CA -> 'letter' or 'legal', most others -> 'a4'), then reorder the
returned UIMenuItem array to put that preferred id first while keeping the
remaining items in the existing order; ensure the function falls back to the
current order if no preference is determined.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:chinchilla -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

**Now: Page-size selection doesn’t appear to influence PDF generation**

You save preference and regenerate, but applyRenderModes/styleToPdf isn’t passed the page size. If Stylize/PDF doesn’t read from global state, page size won’t change.


Patch (if styleToPdf accepts these):

```diff
-      const newPdfDoc = await this.app.stylize.styleToPdf(this.lastRawCode, this.lastLanguageId, {
+      const newPdfDoc = await this.app.stylize.styleToPdf(this.lastRawCode, this.lastLanguageId, {
         fontSize: sizePx,
         lineHeight: lhPx,
         title: this.printTitle,
         theme: this.currentThemeChoice,
+        pageSize: this.getCurrentPageSize(),
+        orientation: this.getCurrentOrientation(),
       });
```

Confirm styleToPdf options support pageSize/orientation:

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
rg -n -C3 'styleToPdf\s*\(' src
rg -n -C3 '\bpage(Size)?\b|\borientation\b' src/PDF.ts src
```

Length of output: 295888

---



</details>

**Page-size changes aren’t applied — stylize.styleToPdf lacks pageSize/orientation**

Verified: Stylize.styleToPdf(opts) does not accept pageSize or orientation (src/Stylize.ts) while PaperPrinter calls styleToPdf (src/PaperPrinter.ts ~140, ~171) and then uses applyRenderModes/updatePdfContentOnly — so changing currentPageSize/currentOrientation won’t affect the generated PDF. PDF.ts reads page size via this.app.paperprinter.getCurrentPageSize(), but the regeneration path here never threads that into styleToPdf.

Action (pick one):
- Mandatory: add pageSize+orientation to styleToPdf opts and implement support in src/Stylize.ts, then update the callers in src/PaperPrinter.ts to pass this.getCurrentPageSize()/getCurrentOrientation() so regenerated PDFs use the new page format.
- OR: make the regeneration path rebuild the PDF via the code in src/PDF.ts (which already reads PaperPrinter prefs) instead of only calling applyRenderModes/updatePdfContentOnly.

Precise places to change: src/Stylize.ts (styleToPdf signature/implementation), src/PaperPrinter.ts (calls at ~lines 137–174 and the selection handlers around 452–519), verify jsPDF initialization in src/PDF.ts uses the passed pageSize/orientation.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/PaperPrinter.ts around lines 137–174 and 452–487 and in src/Stylize.ts
(styleToPdf), the regeneration path updates currentPageSize/currentOrientation
but never passes those values into Stylize.styleToPdf, so regenerated PDFs
ignore new page format; update styleToPdf signature to accept pageSize and
orientation and implement handling in src/Stylize.ts (ensure jsPDF is
initialized with the passed pageSize/orientation or maps them to jsPDF
parameters), then change the callers in src/PaperPrinter.ts (the initial render
calls ~137–174 and the selection handler ~452–487) to pass
this.getCurrentPageSize() and this.getCurrentOrientation() when calling
styleToPdf; alternatively, if you choose the second option, modify the
regeneration path in PaperPrinter to call the PDF rebuild routine in src/PDF.ts
(which already reads PaperPrinter prefs) instead of only calling
applyRenderModes/updatePdfContentOnly, and verify jsPDF initialization in
src/PDF.ts correctly uses the pageSize/orientation getters.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_

**HTML and JS disagree.**

pdf_js targets #pdf-canvas/#loading/#error, but pdf_html removed them. Broken viewer.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In src/PDF.yaml around lines 12–15 the HTML removed the elements that pdf_js
expects (#pdf-canvas, #loading, #error), breaking the viewer; reintroduce those
elements (e.g., a div with id="pdf-canvas" to host canvases, a div with
id="loading" for loading state, and a div with id="error" for error messages) in
the template (positioned logically near the .pdf-pages container) so the
existing pdf_js selectors match the DOM, or alternatively update the JS to use
the new class-based selectors consistently—pick one approach and ensure IDs used
by pdf_js are present in the HTML.
```

</details>

If no saved state, UI.ts injects 'undefined', breaking layout and resize math. Default to a sane pixel value. Fix in UI.ts (see separate comment) or inject a numeric default here.

{{TOOLBAR_LEFT}}px;" which produces "undefinedpx" when TOOLBAR_LEFT is not set;
